PolyMath AI Workshop Series: From Data Manifolds to Causal Inference,polymath-ai-math-foundations-for-robust-ai-systems,"<div class=""container mx-auto px-4 py-8"">

PolyMath AI is an internationally acclaimed, modular course designed for those who want to understand the deep, underlying mathematics of Artificial Intelligence. It offers an immersive learning experience that takes you on a conceptual journey, starting with the geometry of data, moving through probabilistic and statistical learning, and culminating in the fascinating world of causality in AI systems.

This course is perfect for researchers, developers, and academic professionals who are eager to gain a deeper understanding of AI. The blend of rigorous theory and practical demonstrations using Python, PyTorch, and DoWhy ensures that you’ll walk away with the tools and knowledge to think critically and build interpretable, robust, and causally-aware AI systems. You’ll no longer have to rely on AI as a black box—this course empowers you to create AI models that you can truly understand and trust.

The PolyMath AI course aims to bridge the gap between advanced mathematical concepts and practical AI modeling. Through this course, you'll gain the ability to design and evaluate intelligent systems that are grounded in geometry, probabilistic logic, and causal inference, equipping you with the skills to go beyond just empirical results and build more robust, transparent AI systems.

column2: • Understand the Core Concepts: You’ll master the essential mathematical concepts behind AI, from data distributions to causal inference, and apply them to real-world problems.
• Build Robust AI Models: This course helps you design models that aren't just accurate but also reliable and interpretable.
• Science-First AI Approach: Learn how to prioritize ethical, interpretable AI, and develop systems that are based on solid scientific principles.
• Bridge Academia and Application: By the end of this course, you'll have the practical skills needed to apply theoretical knowledge in real-world AI projects.
• Think Like a True AI Scientist: This course fosters the development of thinkers who can reason not just with AI systems, but about them, leading to smarter, more ethical AI solutions.

column1: 📅 Module 1 – Data Foundations & Statistical Learning

column2: • Focus: Hypothesis Testing & Distributions
• AM Session: Dive deep into Kolmogorov-Smirnov tests on spam datasets and explore Bayesian inference for prioritizing real-time data streams.
• Hands-On: Compare Poisson and Gaussian distributions in email patterns and calculate p-values to understand feature significance in spam filtering.

column1: 📅 Module 2 – Model Optimization & Validation

column2: • Focus: ROC Analysis & Gradient Descent
• AM Session: Learn how to derive Fβ-score thresholds for filters and understand Wolfe conditions for setting optimal learning rates in PySpark ML.
• Hands-On: Plot ROC curves and confidence intervals, and explore optimization techniques like line search for decision tree pruning.

column1: 📅 Module 3 – RAG Architecture & Vector Math

column2: • Focus: Topological Data Analysis
• AM Session: Visualize document embeddings with manifold learning and understand the convergence of iterative re-ranking algorithms.
• Hands-On: Use Lagrange multipliers to balance MMR (Maximal Marginal Relevance) and perform persistent homology analysis on knowledge graph connections.

column1: 📅 Module 4 – Multi-Modal Systems & Metrics

column2: • Focus: Measure Theory for AI
• AM Session: Learn Hausdorff distance for cross-modal alignment (e.g., text and sensor data) and explore σ-algebra construction for academic knowledge bases.
• Hands-On: Compute BLEU-4 scores with statistical significance testing and apply optimal transport theory for database alignment.

column1: 📅 Module 5 – Embedded AI & Optimization

column2: • Focus: Convex Relaxation
• AM Session: Understand Karush-Kuhn-Tucker conditions for optimizing edge device power constraints and explore semidefinite programming for model quantization.
• Hands-On: Solve sensor deployment as a traveling salesman problem and optimize FPGA resource allocation with integer linear programming.

column1: 📅 Module 6 – Integrated Capstone & Validation

column2: • Focus: Causal Inference
• AM Session: Learn how to evaluate AI system impact using Do-calculus and conduct Shapley value analysis for multi-component systems.
• Hands-On: Build counterfactual scenarios for edge AI failure modes and perform sensitivity analysis across Big Data → RAG → Edge pipelines.
• Master the Math: Gain a solid understanding of the core mathematical principles behind AI models and how they generalize to new data.
• Visualize High-Dimensional Data: Use geometric tools to analyze and visualize complex, high-dimensional data.
• Develop Robust Models: Learn to build probabilistic models that quantify uncertainty and make robust predictions.
• Apply Causal Inference: Master the use of causal inference to make AI systems more transparent and decision-ready.
• Certificate of Mastery: Receive a certificate that showcases your advanced AI methodology training, demonstrating your expertise to peers and employers.

Hands-On Course: Building a RAG-Powered Q&A Bot,ai-courses-rag-powered-qa-bot,"

About This Course

If you’ve ever wanted a Q&A bot that doesn’t just “guess” answers—but actually looks things up before it responds—this is exactly that. In this hands-on course, you’ll build an intelligent Q&A bot powered by retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), so your bot can pull relevant context from documents and then generate answers grounded in what it finds.

You’ll go end-to-end: set up the project, ingest documents, create embeddings for vector retrieval, and connect everything to a language model so the bot can answer real-time queries in a useful, reliable way. Along the way, you’ll use modern tools like FastAPI, LangChain, FAISS, and OpenAI. And yes—performance matters here, so we’ll also tune for speed and response quality as you iterate.
<hr style=""border: none; border-top: 1px solid #e5e7eb; margin: 18px 0;"" data-start=""1562"" data-end=""1565"" />
Aim

The aim of this course is to equip you to build, deploy, and optimize a RAG-powered Q&A bot—from document ingestion and vector retrieval all the way to integrating language models behind a clean FastAPI interface. In other words: you won’t just understand RAG—you’ll ship a working system.
<hr style=""border: none; border-top: 1px solid #e5e7eb; margin: 18px 0;"" data-start=""1848"" data-end=""1851"" />
Course Objectives
By the end of this course, you will:

column2: • Set up a Python environment and install the dependencies you need to build the project cleanly.
• Ingest documents and generate embeddings so your bot can retrieve information efficiently.
• Implement a vector store and a retrieval function designed for fast, relevant querying.
• Build a QA chain that uses retrieved context to produce better, more grounded answers.
• Expose the bot through a FastAPI endpoint so it can handle real-time queries.
• Practice testing, debugging, and performance optimization so the system holds up under real use.

column1: <hr style=""border: none; border-top: 1px solid #e5e7eb; margin: 18px 0;"" data-start=""2455"" data-end=""2458"" />
Course Structure
Module 1: Environment Setup & Document Ingestion

column2: <li style=""margin: 10px 0;"" data-start=""2542"" data-end=""2866"">

column1: Setting Up the Development Environment:

column2: • Spin up a Python virtual environment so your dependencies stay isolated and predictable.
• Install the core packages using pip (e.g., langchain, faiss-cpu, openai, python-dotenv, fastapi, uvicorn).
• Create a .env file to store your OpenAI API key safely (so it’s not hardcoded into your code).
<li style=""margin: 10px 0;"" data-start=""2867"" data-end=""3169"">
• Write a script to load text or PDF files from your ./docs/ directory.
• Chunk documents into manageable segments (for example, ~500 tokens per chunk) so retrieval stays accurate.
• Generate embeddings (e.g., with OpenAIEmbeddings) and store them in FAISS for fast similarity search.
<li style=""margin: 10px 0;"" data-start=""3170"" data-end=""3275"">
• Preprocess and ingest sample documents so you’re ready for retrieval and QA in the next module.

column1: <hr style=""border: none; border-top: 1px solid #e5e7eb; margin: 18px 0;"" data-start=""3277"" data-end=""3280"" />
Module 2: Vector Store, Retrieval, and QA Chain Implementation

column2: <li style=""margin: 10px 0;"" data-start=""3355"" data-end=""3543"">

column1: Setting Up the Vector Store:

column2: • Initialize FAISS to store vectors and run similarity search.
• Implement a retrieve(query) function that returns the most relevant chunks quickly.
<li style=""margin: 10px 0;"" data-start=""3544"" data-end=""3748"">
• Define a QA chain that uses retrieved context to answer questions more accurately.
• Integrate LLMChain or RetrievalQA (via LangChain) so your pipeline becomes query → retrieve → answer.
<li style=""margin: 10px 0;"" data-start=""3749"" data-end=""3876"">
• Ask 2–3 different questions, inspect the retrieved context, and verify the answer quality (this is where it starts to feel real).

column1: <hr style=""border: none; border-top: 1px solid #e5e7eb; margin: 18px 0;"" data-start=""3878"" data-end=""3881"" />
Module 3: API Endpoint, Testing, and Performance Optimization

column2: <li style=""margin: 10px 0;"" data-start=""3955"" data-end=""4188"">

column1: Building the API Endpoint:

column2: • Scaffold a FastAPI app and expose a /qa POST endpoint for questions.
• Wire retrieval + the chain into the endpoint so your bot answers in real time.
<li style=""margin: 10px 0;"" data-start=""4189"" data-end=""4402"">
• Test with curl or Postman to confirm request/response behavior.
• Handle edge cases (like “no context found”) gracefully—because users will hit them.
<li style=""margin: 10px 0;"" data-start=""4403"" data-end=""4597"">
• Experiment with chunk size and k (top results returned) to balance speed vs. relevance.
• Measure latency and tighten bottlenecks so the bot feels responsive.
<li style=""margin: 10px 0;"" data-start=""4598"" data-end=""4731"">
• Run a full performance check and make targeted tweaks for smoother query handling.

<div style=""width: 100%; margin: 0; font-family: Arial,Helvetica,sans-serif; color: #1f2937; line-height: 1.7;"">
Introduction to the Course

Biology today is data-heavy. Sometimes it’s a handful of microscope measurements, and sometimes it’s millions of DNA reads, time-series sensor data, or species observations collected across years. Either way, you quickly reach a point where spreadsheets feel… cramped.

That’s where Python steps in. It’s a practical, widely-used programming language that helps biologists clean messy datasets, automate repetitive work, analyze results, and turn raw numbers into clear visuals you can actually interpret. In this course, you’ll learn how Python is applied to real biological questions—from genomics and gene expression workflows to ecological modeling and environmental data exploration.

We’ll keep things grounded and hands-on: you’ll write code, work with realistic datasets, and build habits that support reproducible research. By the end, Python won’t feel like “extra work.” It’ll feel like a lab tool you reach for naturally.
Course Objectives

column2: • Understand core Python concepts and where they fit in modern biological research.
• Manipulate biological datasets using libraries such as NumPy, Pandas, and Biopython.
• Visualize patterns and biological phenomena using Matplotlib and Seaborn.
• Get practical exposure to common bioinformatics tasks (e.g., sequence work, expression analysis, basic structure/data workflows).
• Use Python for modeling and statistics to support sound conclusions from experimental data.

Nanotechnology for Energy Storage and Solar Cells,nanotechnology-for-energy-storage-and-solar-cells,"

Introduction to the Course

Nanotechnology for energy storage and solar cells is all about what happens when you “zoom in” to the nanoscale—and start designing materials from the ground up. At that size, surfaces, structures, and charge movement behave differently, which opens the door to batteries that store more energy, charge faster, last longer, and solar cells that convert sunlight more efficiently.

In this course, you’ll explore how nanotechnology is reshaping advanced energy storage systems such as lithium-ion, sodium-ion, and solid-state batteries, along with major photovoltaic technologies including silicon, perovskite, and organic solar cells. We’ll connect the theory to what actually matters in practice: what materials are used, why nanoscale design works, and how researchers and engineers test performance in the real world. By the end, you’ll have a solid practical-and-conceptual toolkit for understanding (and contributing to) the next wave of sustainable energy technology.

column2: • Build a clear foundation in nanotechnology principles and how they apply to energy storage and solar devices.
• Identify key nanomaterials used in electrodes, electrolytes, separators, and photoactive layers.
• Explain how nanostructuring can improve efficiency, stability, lifespan, and overall device performance.
• Understand environmental, health, safety, and regulatory considerations for nano-enabled energy technologies.
• Develop practical insight into designing, fabricating, and evaluating nanoscale energy devices.

column1: Start with the essentials: what “nanoscale” really means, how nanoscale effects change material behavior, and why that matters for batteries and solar cells. Put simply, we’re building the mental model you’ll use for everything that follows.

Dive into the materials that power modern innovation—nanostructured electrodes, quantum dots, nanocomposites, and more. We’ll look at what each material is good at (and where it struggles), because in energy tech there’s always a trade-off… or rather, a design choice.

Learn how performance is measured and improved: characterization tools, common testing methods, and optimization strategies. We’ll also zoom out to the bigger picture—emerging directions, scaling challenges, and where energy nanotechnology is heading next.

Nanotechnology-based digital radiography is a new area of study that combines advanced imaging technology using nanomaterials with traditional digital radiographic systems. As a result, the quality, sensitivity and diagnostic accuracy of the X-ray images produced through this technology are significantly improved by utilizing unique physical and chemical properties of nanomaterials. In addition, X-ray detection efficiency, radiation dose reduction, and high resolution in medical diagnostic imaging are enhanced by using nanotechnology. Digital radiography has been revolutionized by nanotechnology, including more effective detectors, contrast agents, and imaging processing systems using nanomaterials in sympathy with those processes. This course gives detailed knowledge of how to design, fabricate and employ nanomaterials in clinical diagnostic digital radiography. Students will explore both the fundamental principles and real life uses of nanotechnology in radiographic imaging including; cancer diagnosis, musculoskeletal imaging, dental diagnostics and advanced medical imaging.

This course will examine AI-Powered IT Monitoring and Predictive Analytics a cutting-edge means of managing today’s complex IT infrastructure via AI and machine learning. The traditional approach to IT monitoring involves responding to incidents after they have occurred, while AI-driven monitoring takes a proactive approach by using predictive capabilities and automated analysis to generate insights that will help prevent outages and minimize operational expenses. Additionally, this course will cover fundamental concepts of predictive analytics, anomaly detection, and intelligent alerting, as they relate to cloud-based, on-premise, and hybrid IT environments. The course will feature numerous real-life examples of how AI is revolutionizing the way we monitor and maintain our infrastructure for instance from Data Centres, Cloud Platforms, DevOps and Enterprise IT Operations.

Chemical Synthesis of Nanomaterials,chemical-synthesis-of-nanomaterials,"

Introduction to the Course
<div id=""outputText"" class=""output_1_1 text-container"" data-placeholder=""Humanize AI is be ready..."">
<div id=""outputText"" class=""output_1_1 text-container"" data-placeholder=""Humanize AI is be ready..."">

This course offers a comprehensive introduction to the field of Chemical Synthesis of Nanomaterials, an essential area of research and development in nanotechnology. Nanomaterials have unique properties that make them ideal for a wide range of applications, from electronics and energy storage to drug delivery and environmental remediation. This course will focus on the chemical techniques used to synthesize and characterize these materials, exploring the fundamental principles behind their creation and how these methods are applied in real-world applications. By understanding the synthesis processes, participants will gain insights into how nanomaterials can be designed with specific properties to meet the needs of various industries. The course will cover the production of nanoparticles, nanowires, nanotubes, and nanocomposites, along with advanced methods for controlling the size, shape, and functionality of nanomaterials.

Nanomedicine and Targeted Drug Delivery Systems,nanomedicine-and-targeted-drug-delivery-systems,"

Introduction to the Course
<div id=""outputText"" class=""output_1_1 text-container"" data-placeholder=""Humanize AI is be ready..."">

This course will cover the developing field of Nanomedicine and Targeted Drug Delivery Systems an exciting new type of biomedical technology that uses both nanoparticles and nanomaterials to enhance the accuracy and effectiveness of drug delivery. Nanomedicine takes advantage of the different characteristics of nanomaterials, and is changing the way we treat disease with targeted, individualized therapy and with reduced side effects. The course will describe the underlying principles of nanomedicine, how to design and develop nanoparticles for drug delivery, and the clinical application of these new technologies in the treatment of different types of disease, including cancer, neurological diseases and infectious disease. Participants will learn how nanotechnology is transforming drug delivery to targeted cells and tissues resulting in improved treatment response with minimal damage to healthy tissue. This course is appropriate for anyone engaged in pharmaceutical development, biomedical engineering or healthcare innovation.

column2: • Understand the basic principles of nanomedicine and the role of nanotechnology in drug delivery systems.
• Learn the types of nanomaterials (such as liposomes, dendrimers, and quantum dots) used in targeted drug delivery.
• Analyze the regulatory, ethical, and clinical challenges in the development and application of nanomedicines.
• Gain practical skills in designing, characterizing, and testing nanomedicine-based delivery systems for real-world therapeutic applications.
• Explore the biological interactions between nanoparticles and the human body, including cellular uptake, biodistribution, and clearance mechanisms.

column1: Understand what nanomedicine is, the role of nanocarriers, and how their design influences biodistribution.

Learn about toxicity assessment, pharmacokinetics, regulatory frameworks, and real clinical applications of nanomedicines.

Deep Learning for Structural Health Monitoring,deep-learning-for-structural-health-monitoring,"<div class=""container mx-auto px-4 py-8""><!-- Course Header -->

Deep Learning for Structural Health Monitoring is an international, cutting-edge course designed to explore how AI, especially deep learning techniques like Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs), and Transformers, can be applied to monitor the health and integrity of physical structures.

Throughout this course, participants will learn to work with sensor data, vibration signals, thermal imagery, and inspection footage to build deep learning models for detecting cracks, localizing damage, predicting fatigue, and classifying structural conditions. This course bridges the gap between structural engineering, machine learning, and IoT, offering practical tools and deep insights for researchers and professionals in the field.

The aim is to provide participants with both theoretical and practical expertise in applying deep learning techniques to Structural Health Monitoring (SHM), empowering them to carry out early fault detection, predictive maintenance, and safety assurance in civil infrastructure, aerospace, mechanical systems, and smart cities.

Applied Nanobiotechnology for Sustainable Biochemicals and Biofuels,applied-nanobiotechnology-biochemicals-biofuels,"

Introduction to the Course

This course covers Applied Nanobiotechnology for Sustainable Biochemicals and Biofuels which will enable participants to learn how new types of Nanotechnology are changing the way bio-based fuels and chemicals are produced. Because there is a strong desire to move away from fossil fuels, the application of nanobiotechnology has become critical for increasing the overall productivity of biotechnology based processes by improving production efficiencies and decreasing their environmental impact. By integrating nanomaterials and biotechnology, clean, green and efficient biochemical production processes and renewable energy sources can be developed. You will learn about the latest technologies being used with nanomaterials, enzymatic reactions, microbial fuel cells and a variety of other innovative applications. This course will be invaluable to people that work in any area of renewable energy, chemical engineering, bioprocess engineering and sustainability; to anyone that is interested in the options that nanotechnology provides to address global energy issues.

Nano Solutions for Microplastic Crisis,nano-solutions-for-microplastic-crisis,"

Introduction to the Course

The Nano Solutions for Microplastic Crisis course aims to provide participants with modern nanotechnology solutions to a growing problem microplastic contaminants that are infiltrating our environments and food supplies. Innovative solutions are required to effectively lessen the effects of this pervasive environmental problem. This course will give participants in depth knowledge about how nanotechnology is used for detecting, removing, and recycling microplastics from different types of environmental contexts . Participants will be introduced to various methods of using nanomaterials, advanced filtration systems, and chemical treatments to contribute to solving the microplastic crisis. This is a vital learning opportunity for anyone working in environmental science, engineering, policy-making and/or those looking for sustainable options for solving one of the biggest environmental issues in our lifetime.

Refine Your Research – Mastering Manuscript Writing and Referencing Tools,manuscript-writing-referencing-tools,"

Introduction to the Course

The course teaches you how to write a publishable manuscript through your research work while mastering professional reference management. The course provides complete academic writing guidance which includes paper planning, section structuring, clear writing, common error reduction, journal formatting, and modern reference tool usage for creating accurate citations and bibliographies.

column2: • Understand the format and expectations of well-structured research manuscripts.
• Acquire skills to write well-organized, logical, and journal-conforming sections (IMRaD structure).
• Develop methods to enhance academic writing, readability, and scientific narrative skills.
• Become proficient in referencing workflows with the use of Zotero/Mendeley/EndNote-style managers.
• Investigate journal formatting, submission checklists, and typical reviewer demands.
• Create a manuscript draft ready for submission with a well-organized reference library.
• Learn how to build strong scientific papers with well‑organized sections and compelling narratives tailored for scholarly audiences.
• Master citation formats, reference lists, and tools like Mendeley, Zotero, and EndNote to manage sources with ease and accuracy.
• Learn editorial best practices, manuscript polishing strategies, submission checklists, and how to navigate peer review feedback with confidence.
